preamble: The shift from the postalveolar fricative [ʃ] to the labiodental fricative [f] is a form of labialization. It occurs primarily as a developmental process in child language acquisition, where the complex tongue-grooving required for [ʃ] is replaced by the simpler lip-to-teeth gesture of [f].

examples.note: Developmental substitution: replacing a complex sibilant with a simpler labiodental fricative.
Sporadic acoustic substitution influenced by the widespread TH-fronting ([θ] > [f]) in the dialect.
